description: Section 7 (Cross-Role Analysis) shows 0.0s in substep timing and cross_role is null in latest_run.json. Likely swallowed exception in analysis/cross_role.py or PipelineState field not populating. Add explicit logging around cross_role_analysis() call in engine.py.
notes: Blocks verification of Session 7 lowercase fix in prioritize_gaps_by_graph()

description: Total time regressed from 427s baseline to 476.71s after Session 7. Agent 3 (role_comparator) at 265s is the bottleneck. Check whether parallel_workers: 3 is being read from tuning.yaml and passed to ThreadPoolExecutor — may have been dropped when wiring num_ctx options in Session 7.
notes: Session 3 parallelization target was ~100s for Agent 3; 265s means parallel workers may not be active

description: The 1-year internship vs 2-year full-time toggle is only on the left sidebar — easy to miss. Move to main input area above the fold or add a visible callout so users don't accidentally run pipeline on wrong track.
notes: P1 bug flagged by Nathanael during live review

feature: Role recommendation bias / unbiased role expansion
description: When run on non-builder profiles the engine recommended Government Digital Services. Unclear if LLM anchored on builder's requirements during PRD creation. Need visibility into the full role universe considered and a way to expand frame without introducing bias.
notes: Required for engine to be usable with other students' profiles

description: Auto-pull submissions from Tally form gD5Qll and route into CMF pipeline. Eliminates manual PDF upload step for real users. Workflow: pull new submissions -> auto-load resume + WHY -> prompt for LinkedIn PDF -> run pipeline -> save to runs/ with submission ID. Resolve: batch vs webhook; what fields does form collect; PDF vs pasted resume.
notes: Form ID: gD5Qll. Auth pattern in gsc-materials/newsletter-reviews/CRITICAL-LESSON-API-START-DATE.md

description: Show candidate which skills were extracted and by what method (alias / LLM / inferred / transfer_label / graph_inferred). Builds transparency and trust — users need to understand why they have gaps before they accept the analysis.
notes: Tag already exists on skills_flat (match_method field); needs UI layer

description: LLM pass that reads raw resume text and generates 2-3 industry-agnostic labels per described experience. Addresses core problem: candidates describe transferable skills in domain-specific language that misses alias matching. E.g. 'led quarterly business reviews with C-suite' -> ['executive communication' 'stakeholder management' 'executive presence']. Tagged match_method: transfer_label.
notes: New skills.py substep: generate_transfer_labels(). Guard against hallucination with evidence-grounding constraint.

description: Users should be able to re-upload a revised resume and re-run pipeline without starting from scratch. Session state should persist WHY statement and role selection between re-uploads.
notes: Closely related to gap-based follow-up (CMF-009) — iteration loop implies: gap shown -> user refines resume -> re-run

description: Intermediate step after gap identification: ask users contextual questions about whether they actually have flagged skills and what evidence supports them. E.g. coding skill: 'Did you take a class? Build with it? Informal learning?' Competitive intelligence: 'Did you do it extensively in prior roles?' Link directly to the relevant role section in UI.
notes: Also allows UI clickable role links for direct navigation

description: Two additional data inputs: (1) jobs already applied to — context for recommendations, avoids suggesting already-rejected roles; (2) info NOT on resume that user thinks is worth highlighting or has had challenges incorporating. Both parsed separately from resume.
source: Voice memo 2026-02-27-18-58 (text note); PROJECT_LOG.md Feature Backlog
notes: Captured as typed text note mid-voice memo — explicit feature request

description: Agent 1 (profile synthesizer) skill_clusters and industry_signals quality still unverified post-Session 8 schema fix. Agent 2 (motivation extractor) and Agent 3 (role comparator) untested for output quality. Need calibration run on Nathanael's profile as baseline.
notes: Depends on: successful end-to-end run after Session 6/8 fixes verified

description: Validate whether 3 samples in Agent 3 (role_comparator) provides sufficient agreement signal. Check if sampling paths are diverse enough to produce meaningful confidence bands vs all converging on same answer.
Validate composite confidence band scores against human judgment on known profiles. Currently theoretical — weights in confidence.py not calibrated against ground truth.
notes: Requires multiple profile runs as test set

description: Split role display: closest-fit roles (highest % match) vs dream-fit roles (aligned with stated WHY/aspirations). System explains why percentages differ between the two sets — e.g. 'You described wanting X but your experience anchors to Y which matches better for Z roles'.
notes: V2 target

description: Add helper text / example prompts around the WHY statement input field. Currently too open-ended for most users. Guide what kind of content is useful (motivations / career direction / values / aspirations rather than skills).
notes: V2/V3 target per Nathanael

description: Chat-like UI where system asks targeted questions and user responds instead of single text dump. Similar to Claude/ChatGPT engagement pattern. Enhances user engagement and data quality for WHY extraction.
notes: V2 target — significant architectural change to intake flow

description: Render skill_graph.py adjacency data visually in Streamlit. 79 nodes; color by candidate status (green=matched / yellow=inferred / red=missing); edge thickness=co-occurrence weight; click node to see roles. Options: streamlit-agraph (~20-30 lines) or pyvis (richer but needs components.html). Makes graph inference bugs immediately visible.
source: Voice memo — Feb 28 2026 (via PROJECT_LOG.md Feature Backlog)
notes: Data already built; pure UI layer. Neo4j overkill — 79 nodes, no persistent graph DB needed.

description: Add visual elements to candidate report: color-coded sections / icons / emojis / clear styled headings. Makes PDF and on-screen report more polished for sharing with others.
notes: Low effort if done during PDF export work; bundle with CMF-019 completed work

description: Styled 7-section candidate report downloadable as PDF. Download button in app.py alongside JSON export.
notes: Completed Session 9 (Mar 1 2026) via pdf_export.py

description: Rather than auto-generating roles, let users select from role_taxonomy.json list and compare fit across chosen roles. Reduces arbitrariness — 'why these roles' concern. Could complement auto-match as an alternative mode.
notes: V2 target — requires UI overhaul of role input flow

description: GUI layer for adjusting tuning.yaml parameters (weights / thresholds / model selection) without editing YAML directly. Phase 2+ GUI target.
notes: Useful for calibration work once prompt tuning is done

description: Upgrade role_comparator and gap_analyzer from qwen2.5:7b to phi4:14b as reasoning model. Single-line change in tuning.yaml + ollama pull phi4:14b. Research shows 14B-class models outperform 7B on structured reasoning.
source: PROJECT_LOG.md Future Phases; Session 2 Decision Log
notes: Upgrade path already documented in tuning.yaml; blocked on local VRAM availability

description: Use Scite MCP as primary research tool to brainstorm and evaluate approaches for 10x effectiveness improvement: more efficient agent layering / neural network integration / deterministic algorithm improvements / output relevance. North star = extremely relevant + useful + actionable output.
notes: Fellowship P1 strategic priority — precedes architectural decisions for V2

description: Integrate Canvas LMS data as additional context for role matching. Deferred from Phase 1 in favor of skill extraction pipeline. Remains on radar for Phase 2+, but not active until MVP is validated.
notes: Explicitly deferred — revisit after MVP validation with real users
